title: MiFID II Best Execution — Slippage vs Reference Price by Client Segment and Instrument Class

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → 'Art.27'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: OMS fills + market data snapshot service
spl:
index=trading sourcetype="ems:fill" earliest=-30d
| eval slip_bps=abs(price-ref_price)/ref_price*10000
| stats median(slip_bps) as med_slip, perc95(slip_bps) as p95_slip by client_segment, instrument_class, symbol
| where client_segment="retail" AND p95_slip>50
| sort - p95_slip
value: Best execution is measured by outcomes. Trending slippage against a consolidated reference price by retail vs professional clients evidences differentiated quality monitoring required for policy tuning and disclosures.
implementation: (1) Define `ref_price` methodology consistently; (2) exclude auctions; (3) monthly best ex committee pack; (4) integrate with order routing analysis; (5) document outliers investigated.
visualization: Box plot by segment, Table (worst symbols), Single value (breach count).
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
